reviews.review_text: An amazing stress-reliever that stops just short of true realismIf you are looking for a sim game to wind down after a long day, this is perfect. The gameplay loop is very addictive and relaxing. That being said, the mechanics are simplified compared to real-world wrenching. For instance, when doing a timing job, you don't have to worry about top dead center (TDC) or use specialized tools like timing locking rods/pins to hold the engine position. Adding details like piston alignment during assembly would have made it perfect. Highly recommended, but don't expect a 1:1 real life tutorial.
If u know nothing about cars and want to get into them this game is perfect bc i thought it would be very hard but i just got sucked into this game and i cant wait for 2026 to come out

I was just looking for a new puzzle game where I could move pieces and connect them together in a satisfying way, with relaxed pacing. I knew close to nothing about cars. I had very little interest in cars and even less interest in their mechanical parts. I don’t even drive. I would never have guessed that I’d enjoy this game so much. 

It is definitely not for everyone, but if it’s for you, you’ll love it.
great game better on pc because of mods and my girlfriend left me because i wouldnt get off the game 10-10 game
An amazing game with no time pressure, allowing you to fully immerse yourself in the details and get lost fixing up cars

I really liked CMS 2018 at the time. Felt nostalgic and decided to play the latest available one and honestly I am quite disappointed to see it feels more like an UI update to CMS 2018 than a new game in its own. Everything is or feels recycled from the old version. Which I understand is acceptable but only to a certain degree. It just feels wrong after 3 years of development to have everything sounding and feeling like the old game.

In short there is nothing new compared to CMS 2018 which is disappointing.
This game is the perfect mechanical world to get lost in while listening to a book or the like.

Everything except the level/game design is good.

The design mistakes here are unbelievable.

1. Repairing costs money.
I worked as a mechanic for 3 years, and repairing isolated parts never cost a penny. This system almost kills the repair mechanic. At least instant repairs save it a little.

2. The barn mechanic feels dead.
If there is supposed to be profit, the game should show the expected profit on the part. Otherwise, there is no reason to use this mechanic.

3. The junkyard mechanic also feels dead.
The costs seem to be determined mostly by durability percentage. If I am not going to make a profit, why would I go to the junkyard and dig for parts? The game needs better indicators.

4. Building cars from scrap feels dead.
Again, pricing seems too dependent on durability percentage. I make more profit from storyline missions, so building cars from scrap does not feel worth it.

Also, please stop giving treasure chests from treasure chests. It is very annoying.

I came here to write this because Car Mechanic Simulator 2018 was better than this. As a game developer, I can see lazy design decisions here.

So I got this game on sale for $4 and enjoyed myself for 22 hours. So I actually do recommend it as a mild entertainment for a sale price of say $15 or less. Why the thumbs down? This:

I played an earlier version of this years ago, and my same complaints exist. It is clear that the developer is just recycling the same idea and play style, updating the graphics a bit, and releasing a new version. Here are my specific gripes:

1 - The only damage car parts ever have is rust. The parts have a percent value, and the lower it is, the more rust. This is a lazy use of a texture and ignores all the other reasons that a car or car part might be damaged. What about the low hanging fruit of accidents? There are no accident damaged cars which is crazy. They try to simulate this by having some missing parts, but again, it's just lazy. Where are the dents, scratches, tears, cracks, etc? In a visual inspection you are just looking for parts with more rust then the others and that is it. That gets boring fast.

2 - Quality of life issues. When you buy a part, you have to go into a store, find the part (at least now you have a shopping list and can click the part there), click on the part to bring up a new screen with nothing useful on it, click to buy, and move on to the next part. Lots of unnecessary clicks and grindy. Then remove the parts you bought from your shopping list one at a time, and don't miss the little X buttons or it just closes the whole list. Also why are all electronic parts in a separate store so you have to keep going back and forth between the two stores. Real car part stores are not separated like this. Not to mention different stores for rims and tires?? Body parts and internal parts? Why can't we just click on the part in the shopping list and go to that part wherever it is? Why is there no upgrade to buy that allows you to just buy everything on the shopping list in one swoop? Or buy by clicking on the part while working on the car? When buying a license plate, why can't we type in what we want, instead of using arrows and clicks to change each letter? The tutorial is just some text and photos, and sucks. Many things are not explained. You have to buy all parts for a repair, and finish the whole thing before you end the repair mission. You have no way to know what parts you will need before accepting the mission. If you run out of money, you can't complete the mission. There is no way to get a loan, or an advance from the customer. You are just screwed. Except for body parts, the parts give you no description of what car or engine they go with, so you can't shop for parts for a specific car or engine. They have barn and junk yard searches, which are useless because you never know what parts will go with the car you are working on.

3 – Boring and stupid mini games. When doing some things like repairing parts, they just give you a stupid mini game where you try to click when an arrow is over a green bar. Very lazy development on the parts of the game that don't show in the trailer videos. What about actually grinding off rust, soaking in oil and solvents, welding cracks, etc? A whole interesting area of the game and a game play loop that is just glossed over with a dumb mini game.

4 – No game at all in some upgrades. When you buy the welder, car wash, and paint shop, there is no game play at all. You just click, agree to the price, and it's done. No hands on spraying? No ability to paint specific parts. No actual welding. Lazy. Diagnostic tools just put up a display and make some noise and then parts are listed with their quality values. No plugging in and hooking up the tool and running the engine. No interpretation of the results.  Removing the engine with a crane just happens in a blink. No animation or hooking things up. They made an animation to drain oil, and then just got to lazy to do anything else.

5 – Upgrades don't effect customers. You buy a welder so you can fix frames. But no customers come in wanting that service. You buy the paint shop, but no customers come wanting paint. The story missions eventually require you to buy these things, and then I think after doing them you might get customers needing that (I didn't play long enough) but there is no reason to buy them until a story mission requires it.

There are other things, more then I can remember at the moment. But the bottom line is that love and thought are not being put into the development of these games. Each one just recycles as much as it can from previous games, and includes as little individual development (and cost) as possible. The whole thing rubs me the wrong way and I don't like it.
its a pretty good game, the repairing is fun and sometimes difficult i like the new scraping system, i give 7/10 good game

Good game, but compared to the previous 2017, and the upcoming 2026, I would recommend waiting a few months and getting the better game, This one is a lot of cutscenes, making things such as leaving the garage and checking parking inconvenient.

[h1] CMS21 is fine... its not great at anything its . . .fine [/h1]

I wont give it a negative because honestly it does what it says on the tin. Fair enough I guess. 

BUT. . . 

Economic modelling is weak so its far too easy to just make boat loads on money. In a game that loosely "simulates" running a garage, some sort of economic challenge would be nice. 

No Multiplayer. This honestly baffles me,  why WHY can you not fix cars with your friends ?? 

Every car is basically the same in the way it comes apart even the DLC cars which mostly reuse the same mechanical models. 

Like I said, its not bad, so not negative but its not good enough.

Here's hoping the 2026 version feels more complete.

Got this on sale with all DLC for less than $35. If you enjoy cars in any form, you will love this game. I had already owned the game and some DLC on Xbox but decided to repurchase on steam to gain access to mods and get all of the DLC. With all that has been added to the game both DLC and mods, I am having an absolute blast. Started from the ground up all over again and am on my way to become a millionaire with a few more restorations under my belt. I personally find it to be extremely relaxing to rebuild cars at my own pace and use my creativity in process. There are so many potential things you could do and build. Just to be perfectly clear though, I would only recommend this game if you are a big fan of cars. If you're a fan of Forza, I think you will love this game too!

Car Mechanic Simulator 2021: The Ultimate Automotive Obsession

The Mastery of the Wrench
What makes CMS2021 so brilliant is its accessibility. The user interface and the radial menus make what should be a complicated, overwhelming task feel completely intuitive.

The Diagnostic Loop: I love the puzzle aspect. A customer brings in a car that "makes a funny noise," and it’s up to you to use the compression testers, OBD scanners, and test tracks to figure out what's wrong.

The Upgraded Sandbox: The 2021 edition brought massive improvements over the older titles, like fluid draining, minigames for fixing parts, and a much more dynamic salvaging system. On PC in 2026, zooming in on highly detailed brake pads, suspension components, and exhaust systems looks incredibly crisp and realistic.

The DLC Fleet: A Car Lover's Dream
Having every single DLC completely transforms the endgame. You aren't just working on generic replicas anymore; you are handling officially licensed masterpieces.

The Icons: Tearing apart a legendary Nissan Skyline GT-R, restoring a classic Ford Mustang, or fine-tuning a high-end Porsche or Pagani adds a whole new layer of excitement to the grind.

The Drag Racing DLC: This was a game-changer for the 100% run. It didn't just give you cars; it gave you a purpose for tuning them. Choosing the right gear ratios, adjusting ECU stages, and taking your custom builds to the drag strip to hunt down achievements added a high-octane competitive edge that the base game lacked.

The 100% Completion: From Scrap to Supercar
To get every achievement, you have to prove you are a true master mechanic.

The Barn Find Renovations: Buying a completely rusted-out shell from a barn or an auction, spending hours sandblasting the rust, replacing 100% of the parts with brand-new performance gear, and selling it for a massive profit is the ultimate gameplay loop.

The Level 50 Grind: Maxing out your level and fully upgrading your garage—unlocking the paint shop, the dyno, and the extra lifts—takes serious dedication. It turns your humble little repair shop into a world-class automotive factory.

The Verdict
Car Mechanic Simulator 2021 is a masterpiece of the simulation genre. It strikes the perfect balance between realistic mechanical accuracy and fun, rewarding video game progression. With all the DLCs included, it is an absolute juggernaut of content that every car enthusiast needs to 100%.

Final Thought: If you`ve ever had doubts about this game, don`t. Just play it, and you`ll fall in love with it.
